title: Code in always_ff behaves incorrectly
body:
We found a possible bug in the process of locating a problem. The state transition of a state machine is in the always_ff statement，Assigned from NS to CS, but from the waveform it looks like NS is assigned to CS. This is the opposite of running on vcs.

Part of the code is as follows：

206    always_ff @(posedge clk, negedge rst_n)
207    begin
208       if(~rst_n)
209       begin
210           CS                       <= DISABLED_ICACHE;
211           fetch_addr_Q             <= '0;
212           fetch_req_Q              <= '0;
213           fetch_way_Q              <= '0;
214
215           counter_FLUSH_CS         <= '0;
216
217       end
218       else
219       begin
220           **CS <= NS;**
221           counter_FLUSH_CS         <= counter_FLUSH_NS;
222
223           if(save_fetch_way)
224             fetch_way_Q              <= fetch_way_int;
225
226           if(enable_pipe)
227           begin
228                fetch_req_Q    <= 1'b1;
229                fetch_addr_Q   <= fetch_addr_i;
230           end
231           else  if(clear_pipe)
232                 begin
233                      fetch_req_Q <= '0;
234                 end
235
236       end
237    end

wave generated by Verilator ：
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/43007864/78759678-94742280-79b2-11ea-94b9-67d5972e19cd.png)

wave generated by VCS：
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/43007864/78759753-b1105a80-79b2-11ea-8bdd-a139f7199c9a.png)
